passing_score_scale: Scaled score 25–75 for RDN (passing = 25); scaled for NDTR
exam_format: Computer-adaptive at Pearson VUE test centers

testing_centers.notes: Computer-adaptive exams administered at Pearson VUE test centers. Schedule through Pearson VUE after CDR eligibility authorization is received.

id_requirements: Government-issued photo ID required. Name on ID must match registration exactly.
exam_rules: No personal items, notes, or reference materials permitted at Pearson VUE test centers. Both exams are computer-adaptive (CAT format). 45-day waiting period between failed attempts. Maximum 5 total attempts permitted; candidates exhausting attempts require CDR re-evaluation before additional attempts are granted. Credentials must be renewed every 5 years by earning 75 CPEUs (Continuing Professional Education Units).
renewal_options: RDN and NDTR credentials must be renewed every 5 years by earning 75 CPEUs (Continuing Professional Education Units). Renewal managed through CDR at cdrnet.org.

retake_policy.notes: 45-day waiting period between failed attempts. Maximum 5 total attempts; after exhausting attempts, CDR re-evaluation is required before further testing is permitted.

domains.name: Principles of Dietetics (nutrition science, food science)
Nutrition Care for Individuals and Groups
Management of Food and Nutrition Programs and Services

official_study_resources.title: CDR Exam Candidate Handbook
official_study_resources.notes: Official candidate handbook and content outlines available at cdrnet.org
